mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment above 5.5% over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns as investor confidence weakens.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-unemployment regimes exceed 1.5% despite the signal or the trigger condition fails to correlate with returns (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and housing starts indicates strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to predict SPX 60d returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or returns are negative in 60%+ of cases despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle, indicated by rising FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in tightening regimes exceed 1.5% or the trigger condition shows no correlation with returns (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 105 over 2 months signals weak supply chain activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns as corporate profits face downward pressure.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in low-INDPRO regimes exceed 1% or the trigger condition fails to predict returns (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in residential construction permits reflects strong demand for housing, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-HOUST regimes fall below 0.5% or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in unemployment below 4.5% signals a tight labor market, generating positive S&P 500 returns as wage growth fuels consumer spending and corporate margins.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in low-unemployment regimes fall below 2% or the trigger condition shows no correlation with returns (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) combined with low rates (FEDFUNDS < 3%) creates uncertainty, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days as investors hedge against stagflation risks.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in stagflation scenarios exceed 1.5% or the interaction between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict returns (Rank IC < 0.03).
